fix: onDowngrade — пропускать SQLDelight→SQLDelight даунгрейд

При откате с версии 4 (break) на версию 3, обработчик пытался
пересоздать таблицу Train во всех БД (включая Settings.db, где
Train не существует). Теперь Room→SQLDelight миграция запускается
только при oldVersion >= 14.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: all frontend tests green (97/97, 4 suites)

Three fixes found during Commit-AT build — all caught by the test pipeline:

1. CascadeView.tsx: Convert inline fetch to useEffect with AbortController.
   Prevents setLoading(false) firing after component unmounts (act() warning
   that caused the suite to report as failed despite all tests passing).
   Also fixes a real memory leak in production — fetch was not cancelled
   on unmount before this change.

2. CommitPane.test.tsx: Add missing api.config mock (import.meta.env error)
   and mock React Query hooks (useDeliveryScore, useStreak, useCompetencyLevel)
   so tests don't require QueryClientProvider.

3. setup.ts: Add afterEach(act) to flush any remaining React state updates
   between tests, preventing cross-test contamination.

All 66 backend (xUnit) + 97 frontend (Jest) tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve lowercase 'Loi du 1er août 2018' identifier for A2 fleet audit

Three-part fix for the A2 audit failure on Luxembourg data protection law:

1. Abbreviation map — adds 'loi du 1er août 2018' (and variants: uppercase,
   unaccented 'aout', LPD/lpd) → 'loi-2018-08-01-a686' so Step 2 of the
   resolution cascade catches the lowercase identifier immediately.

2. Parser fix — parser.ts was not traversing <title> structural containers
   (Legilux uses <title> for top-level "Titre I / II" divisions instead of
   <part>), causing loi-2018-08-01-a686 to yield 0 provisions during
   ingestion. Adds 'title' and 'subsection' to the isArray list and adds
   processContainer branches for both element types. Now yields 77 provisions.

3. DB patch mechanism — loi-2018-08-01-a686 was missing from the released
   database entirely. Adds:
   - data/seed/loi-2018-08-01-a686.json (77 provisions from Legilux XML)
   - scripts/patch-db.ts: inserts seed overrides into the downloaded DB
   - vercel.json: runs patch-db between download-db and build
   - package.json: adds patch-db script and includes data/seed in files
   - .gitignore: un-ignores data/seed/ so seed overrides can be committed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

🎯 FIX CRITIQUE: Parser id en integer pour UPDATE Supabase

PROBLÈME TROUVÉ:
- toggleMenuItem recevait id comme STRING ("24")
- PostgreSQL ne matchait pas string "24" avec integer 24
- UPDATE modifiait 0 lignes
- Aucun événement Realtime déclenché

SOLUTION:
- parseInt(id, 10) pour forcer integer
- Parser newValue en boolean (true/false)
- Maintenant l'UPDATE matche et le realtime se déclenche

Add proximity damping to EMFVelocityModifier for opposite-charge NPCs

Port viscous braking from EMFPhysicsProp to prevent NPCs with opposite
charge from snapping through the player. When NPC enters the cutoff zone,
EM force drops to zero and velocity is damped exponentially (k=10/s).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Stop building vendored n8n in Coolify — Activepieces is the workflow engine

INCLUDE_VENDORED_N8N was still set to true, causing every Coolify deploy to
clone the n8n repo and run a full pnpm build. Since we migrated to Activepieces
(flow.wickedlab.io) as the ops runtime, n8n is no longer needed at build time.
Setting to false skips the entire n8n build stage and cuts deploy time significantly.

commit.message:
Major quality overhaul: fix critical bugs, add features, PWA support

Critical fixes:
- Fix coordinate conversion for text/draw/highlight saving (renderScale)
- Fix draw canvas position drift on scroll
- Fix highlight overlay alignment to PDF canvas
- Fix PDF viewer background to match dungeon theme

New features:
- Japanese font support (Noto Sans JP via fontkit)
- Undo/redo for annotations
- Annotation list panel with delete
- Pinch-to-zoom on mobile
- Double-tap to fit width
- Keyboard shortcuts (arrow keys for page nav)
- PWA manifest + icons for installable app
- Beforeunload warning for unsaved changes

UX improvements:
- Text cursor indicator (crosshair + red marker)
- Continuous text addition without closing panel
- Draw stroke count + cancel with confirmation
- Highlight marching ants animation + size display
- Auto-fill filename in save panel
- File size display during loading
- Zoom presets (50%, 200%)
- Better error messages for corrupt/password PDFs

Quality:
- ARIA labels + roles for accessibility
- WCAG AA color contrast compliance
- React.memo + useMemo for annotation rendering
- Non-blocking thumbnail generation

commit.message:
Fix click-collect modal: use server-side rendered detection

Replace AJAX-template-dependent tr[data-pickup-only] check with a
server-side rendered [%cart_items%] block that outputs a hidden trigger
element (#js-has-pickup-only) when any item has misc32=N. This avoids
timing issues with AJAX template loading.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: race condition en fuentes web al procesar preguntas en paralelo

lastWebSources era una variable de instancia compartida que se
sobrescribía entre preguntas al procesarse 15 en paralelo, causando
que las fuentes web y rankings no coincidieran con la respuesta.
Ahora generateContentWithProviderAndSources devuelve las fuentes
directamente, eliminando la dependencia del estado compartido.
También limpia logs de debug temporales de Gemini.

fix: add date prefix to tracked tasks for cleaner organization

Tasks now auto-prefixed with [MM-DD] so the Claude project
stays organized chronologically without needing subfolders.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix mesocycle progress stuck at W1/D1: repair all recent orphaned logs (#337)

The repair banner detection and repair action both only handled logs
from a single orphaned mesocycle ID. Now:
- Detection: triggers when current mesocycle has 0 logs but recent logs
  (dated after mesocycle start) point to ANY other mesocycle ID
- Repair: migrates ALL recent logs to current mesocycle positionally,
  regardless of which old mesocycle ID they point to

Co-authored-by: ibrahim allaoui <ibrahimallaoui@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Hábitos: fixa CDN Supabase na versão 2.39.3 com UMD (fix window.supabase undefined)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: recalculate times and routes after reorder, remove, ±duration, and chat edits

Previously, reordering activities only changed the array order without
updating time_start/time_end or route_to_next. Similarly, removing an
activity or adjusting duration only updated the single activity without
cascading time changes to subsequent activities.

Changes:
- Add _recalculate_times() helper that reassigns sequential times for
  all activities based on durations and travel times between them
- Reorder endpoint: recalculates times + recomputes routes via Google
  Routes API distance matrix after reordering
- Remove action: recalculates times for remaining activities
- Adjust duration: cascades time changes to all subsequent activities
- Chat day plan edit: recomputes routes via distance matrix after LLM
  returns updated activities (new activities get proper route data)
- Inject DayPlanOrchestrator into reorder + chat endpoints for route
  computation access

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Generalize host feature to support multiple injected fields

Replace the single [host] config section with [[injected-fields]], which
supports multiple static columns injected into all SQL output. Each
injected field adds a column to INSERT statements and a condition to
DELETE/UPDATE WHERE clauses.

- Rename proto Host -> InjectedField, change patch field to repeated
- Rename HostConfig -> InjectedFieldConfig, Option -> Vec
- Rename HostInfo -> InjectedField in SQL generation, Option -> slice
- Add duplicate name validation for injected fields
- Add multi-field acceptance tests
- Update README.md and CONTRIBUTING.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent zombie delegations — crash recovery, watchdog, try/finally

Problem: Kuro restart left orphan processes running, forge slots locked,
and delegation results silently lost. Async throw in close handler could
permanently jam the task queue.

Changes:
- Persist active delegation PIDs to delegation-active.json (state file)
- recoverStaleDelegations() on startup: kill orphans, release forge slots
- watchdogDelegations() each cycle: SIGKILL tasks stuck beyond timeout+30s
- Restructure close handler with try/finally — cleanup ALWAYS runs
  (activeTasks.delete, dequeueNext, removeFromStateFile)
- Error handler also clears state file

Safety chain: state file → startup recovery → watchdog → try/finally

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix stuck todo spinners and orphaned spinners on abnormal agent exit

When the agent loop exits abnormally (internet drop, API timeout, crash),
todos in "doing" status would stay stuck spinning forever. This adds
two layers of cleanup:

1. Agent layer: _reset_stuck_todos() in run_loop.py's finally block
   resets any "doing" todos back to "todo" on every exit path
2. UI layer: message_processor.py stops orphaned spinners and refreshes
   the todo panel after each query completes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Gemini Live API mode for real-time voice + screen CUA

Implements Phase 1 from CONTEXT.md: replaces the request-response
pipeline (STT -> Cloud Run -> TTS) with a single Gemini Live API
streaming session.

New --live flag: `python -m client --live`

- client/live/session.py: Core session manager — opens persistent
  Live API WebSocket, streams mic audio, sends screen context,
  handles function-call-based tool execution (click, type, scroll,
  open_app, press_key)
- client/live/audio.py: Mic capture (16kHz) + speaker playback
  (24kHz) for Live API's native audio
- client/live/screen.py: JPEG screen capture for efficient context
- client/live/tools.py: CUA function declarations + system prompt
- Updated config, pyproject.toml, .env.example for Live API settings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Add scripts structure verification and enhanced sync logging

Enhancements:
1. Scripts Directory Structure Verification
   - Verifies all critical directories synced: architecture/, 01-sync-system/, etc.
   - Detects FAILED structure syncs (missing directories = critical error!)
   - BLOCKS on missing critical directories (doesn't silently fail)
   - Checks: architecture, 01-sync-system, 02-standards-system, 03-execution-system, testing

2. Enhanced Sync Logging
   - Shows directory structure being created during sync
   - Displays key subdirectories synced (architecture/01, 02, 03, etc.)
   - Distinguishes between 'new files synced' vs 'already up-to-date'
   - Tracks subdirectory creation for transparency

3. Documentation
   - Added ASCII diagram showing complete synced structure
   - Lists all 226+ files being synced (root + architecture)
   - Explains why complete structure MUST be synced (helper modules)
   - Documents the strip_prefix logic for path preservation

4. Critical Safety Check
   - If architecture directories don't exist after sync, returns FALSE
   - Prevents running with incomplete/corrupted script structure
   - Fails fast with clear error messages about missing directories

Impact:
- ✅ Scripts sync now verified end-to-end
- ✅ Cannot silently skip subdirectories anymore
- ✅ Directory structure integrity guaranteed
- ✅ Visible logging shows what's being synced
- ✅ System detects corrupt/incomplete syncs immediately

Verified: explorer confirmed policies/ and scripts/architecture/ structures match 99%
Only difference: 1 empty github-integration/ folder (not functional, non-blocking)

Fix rate limiting tests — duplicate policy name error (#13)

The test factory used AddPolicy with the same name as Program.cs, causing
ArgumentException. Fixed by using UseSetting to override config values
that the existing policy reads at startup.
